rag_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL component type or implementation scopes based on the scope of the referenced ports and bindings.
AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax and should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees in AGREE should align with the data types and port directions defined in the AADL model.
Helper declarations in AGREE must match the actual types of the referenced expressions to avoid type mismatches.
Boolean expressions in AGREE should only be used when the AADL port is explicitly typed as bool; avoid unsafe conversions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are used for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens (ms, Hz) as AGREE variables.
Do not use subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing properties
Implementation-level bindings and assignments in component types
Model fusion and scope preservation rules
